records.ety: [Originally the same word as shade. √162. See Shade.]

defs: Shade within defined limits; obscurity or deprivation of light, apparent on a surface, and representing the form of the body which intercepts the rays of light; as, the shadow of a man, of a tree, or of a tower. See the Note under Shade, n., 1.

quotes: Night's sable shadows from the ocean rise.

defs: A shaded place; shelter; protection; security.
quotes: In secret shadow from the sunny ray, On a sweet bed of lilies softly laid.

defs: A reflected image, as in a mirror or in water.
That which follows or attends a person or thing like a shadow; an inseparable companion; hence, an obsequious follower.
A spirit; a ghost; a shade; a phantom.
An imperfect and faint representation; adumbration; indistinct image; dim bodying forth; hence, mystical representation; type.
quotes: The law having a shadow of good things to come.
[Types] and shadows of that destined seed.

defs: An uninvited guest coming with one who is invited.
quotes: I must not have my board pastered with shadows That under other men's protection break in Without invitement.

records.ety: [OE. shadowen, AS. sceadwian. See adow, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shadowed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shadowing.]

defs: To cut off light from; to put in shade; to shade; to throw a shadow upon; to overspead with obscurity.
quotes: The warlike elf much wondered at this tree, So fair and great, that shadowed all the ground.

defs: To conceal; to hide; to screen.
quotes: Let every soldier hew him down a bough. And bear't before him; thereby shall we shadow The numbers of our host.

defs: To protect; to shelter from danger; to shroud.
quotes: Shadowing their right under your wings of war.

defs: To mark with gradations of light or color; to shade.
To represent faintly or imperfectly; to adumbrate; hence, to represent typically.
quotes: Augustus is shadowed in the person of Æneas.

defs: To cloud; to darken; to cast a gloom over.
quotes: The shadowed livery of the burnished sun.
Why sad? I must not see the face O love thus shadowed.

defs: To attend as closely as a shadow; to follow and watch closely, especially in a secret or unobserved manner; as, a detective shadows a criminal.
